title: sphaeralcea crispa
intro: Sphaeralcea crispa, also known as Desert Marigold, is a drought-tolerant, flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally, Desert Marigold has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can aid in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant's medicinal properties include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and boost the immune system, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Desert Marigold can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in infusions, capsules, and topical applications to harness its health benefits.
